fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but currently unprofitable, with a high forward P/E and significant analyst growth estimates.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — substantial future growth expected.
Forward P/E 39.46x, PEG ratio 0.78x — valuation may be justified by high growth.
Current ratio 3.83x and Quick ratio 2.85x — strong short-term liquidity.
Total cash $439.70M vs Total debt $280.42M — manageable leverage.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — currently loss-making.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor profitability metrics.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Debt/Equity 25.36 — high leverage relative to equity.
Mean analyst target $151.30 vs spot $188.20 — current target implies downside.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is experiencing strong demand from data centers and AI computing, highlighted by a significant upgrade from Mediacom for one million homes. However, the stock saw a notable pullback recently, alongside other optics players, as the sector cools off. Despite the volatility, analysts maintain a buy consensus, with a mean target suggesting upside from current levels, though the company is still navigating profitability challenges.
